name: HMS St. Martin (W 27)
events: 24 Jan 1941 HMS Tigris (Lt.Cdr. H.F. Bone, DSO, DSC, RN) conducted gunnery exercises in the Clyde area on a target towed by HMS St. Martin. (1)

26 Aug 1943 HMS Stonehenge (Lt. D.S.M. Verschoyle-Campbell, DSC and Bar, RN) conducted A/S exercises in the Clyde area with aircraft from HMS Tracker (Capt.(Retd.) D.S. McGrath, RN). Also a practice attack was made on Tracker. These exercises were followed by night gunnery exercises on a target that was being towed by HMS St. Martin. (2)
18 Feb 1944 HMS Strongbow (Lt. J.A.R. Troup, DSC, RN) conducted gunnery exercises in the Clyde area on a target that was being towed by HMS St. Martin. (3)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
